extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 3–4 time steps the series starts at a moderate value (around 0.2–0.35) and clearly decreases.
R2: From about time 4–5 to time 11–12 the series stays low (near 0–0.1) and then rises steeply.
R3: The main peak (maximum value) occurs relatively early, around time 13–16.
R4: After this early peak the series decreases steadily, without forming a second, later peak higher than the first.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 5–6 time steps the series stays very low (near 0, generally below 0.1) with little change.
R2: From about time 6–7 to time 12–13 the series rises from this low baseline to much higher values.
R3: The main peak (maximum value) occurs later than in class 0, around time 16–19.
R4: After this later peak the series decreases towards the end, again without any earlier peak higher than this main one.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: At the very beginning (first 2–3 time steps) the values are relatively high (around 0.15–0.30), not close to zero.  
R2: From the start to about time step 6–7 the series shows a clear decreasing trend, reaching a shallow minimum around 0–0.05.  
R3: After this minimum, there is a strong, sustained increase that peaks around time steps 12–18 at high values (approximately 0.6–0.8).  
R4: After the peak, the values decrease steadily but remain clearly above zero for most of the remaining time steps.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: At the very beginning (first 2–3 time steps) the values are very close to zero (around 0–0.05).  
R2: These near-zero values persist longer than in Class 0, typically up to about time step 5–7 before clearly rising.  
R3: After this flat/near-zero segment, there is a sharp increase leading to a high peak around time steps 12–18 (approximately 0.6–0.8).  
R4: After the peak, the values decrease steadily, similar in shape and level to Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Early segment (t ≈ 0–5) starts around 0.2–0.3 and decreases toward a small but clearly positive value (not close to zero).
R2: Middle segment (t ≈ 5–12) shows a strong monotonic-like rise from this small positive level up to a peak around 0.6–0.7.
R3: After the first peak, the series stays relatively high and forms a broad plateau/secondary bump (values mostly above 0.4) before clearly declining.
R4: Late segment (t ≈ 20–24) ends at a moderate value around 0.2–0.4 (not near zero).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Early segment (t ≈ 0–5) is very close to zero, fluctuating around 0.0–0.05, clearly lower than in Class 0.
R2: Middle segment (t ≈ 5–12) rises sharply from near-zero to a peak around 0.6–0.7, similar height to Class 0.
R3: After the peak, the series may show one or more smaller bumps, but values tend to decrease steadily overall.
R4: Late segment (t ≈ 20–24) ends very close to zero again (around 0.0–0.1), notably lower than the ending values of Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first quarter of the series (early time steps), values start moderately high (around 0.2–0.3) and clearly decrease toward near-zero.  
R2: Around the middle of the series, values rise sharply from near-zero up to a single dominant peak of about 0.6–0.75.  
R3: After this main peak, values stay relatively high but form only gentle undulations (no second strong, narrow spike).  
R4: Toward the end of the series, values decrease smoothly from moderate/high levels down to about 0.1–0.2, not to near-zero.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first quarter of the series, values stay very close to zero (flat low baseline, typically below about 0.05).  
R2: From this flat start, values then rise steeply in the middle to a strong main peak (about 0.6–0.75).  
R3: Often a second noticeable local peak occurs near or just after the main peak, creating a more “spiky” mid-region than in Class 0.  
R4: In the last quarter of the series, values fall from moderate levels all the way down to near-zero (below about 0.05).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series, values start moderately high (around 0.15–0.35) and then decrease toward a small trough near 0.0.  
R2: Around the middle (roughly indices 9–13), the series rises very steeply from near 0.0 up to a high peak (about 0.6–0.7).  
R3: After this peak, values stay relatively high but form a broad “plateau” or secondary hump (still above ~0.4) before declining.  
R4: In the last quarter, the series decreases from that high region but remains clearly above zero (not returning to the very low near‑zero baseline).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first third of the series, values stay very close to zero (flat near baseline, typically below ~0.05) with little variation.  
R2: From this flat baseline, the series then rises steeply to a main peak (about 0.6–0.75) around the middle indices.  
R3: After the peak, the series declines more or less monotonically without forming a sustained high plateau; it briefly passes mid‑levels (0.3–0.5) and continues downward.  
R4: By the final points, values have dropped back close to the near‑zero baseline (typically below ~0.1).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a relatively high value (around 0.2–0.35) and steadily decreases during the first 6–8 time steps.
R2: After this early decrease, the series stays near a low plateau (close to 0) for several time steps (roughly until time 8–9).
R3: From this low plateau, the series then rises sharply to a single main peak between times 12–18, reaching around 0.6–0.8.
R4: After the main peak, the series decreases smoothly and monotonically toward lower values until the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a very low value (near 0) and stays almost flat and close to 0 during the first 5–7 time steps.
R2: From this flat low start, the series then rises gradually (not sharply) up to time about 10–11.
R3: After this gradual rise, the series increases more steeply to a main peak between times 13–18, reaching around 0.6–0.8.
R4: Following the main peak, the series decreases toward the end, with the decline beginning from a somewhat higher and less smooth plateau compared with Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the very early segment (indices ≈0–3), values are relatively high, typically above 0.1.  
R2: From indices ≈3–7, the series dips to its minimum, usually very close to 0.  
R3: From indices ≈7–13, the series rises steeply from near 0 to a clear main peak around 0.6–0.8.  
R4: After the main peak (indices ≈13–23), the series declines but remains mostly above 0.2 until the very end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the very early segment (indices ≈0–3), values are very low and flat, typically below 0.05.  
R2: From indices ≈3–8, the series stays near its minimum, with only a slight rise and still below about 0.07.  
R3: From indices ≈8–13, the series rises steeply from near 0 to a main peak around 0.6–0.8.  
R4: After the main peak (indices ≈13–23), the series declines steadily from high values back toward near 0 by the end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: During the first quarter of the series (roughly indices 0–6), values are relatively high (around 0.15–0.35) and clearly above zero, often decreasing from the start.
R2: After this initial part (roughly indices 6–10), the series approaches a low point near zero before rising.
R3: The main peak occurs in the middle (roughly indices 12–18) with maximum value around 0.65–0.8.
R4: After the main peak, the series decreases but typically stays at a moderate level (>0.25) for some time before finally dropping towards the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: During the first quarter of the series (roughly indices 0–6), values stay very close to zero (around 0–0.05) with little variation.
R2: After this flat near-zero start, the series rises steadily from low values to the main peak.
R3: The main peak occurs in the middle (roughly indices 12–18) with maximum value around 0.6–0.8.
R4: After the peak, the series decreases more directly and smoothly towards low values at the end, without a long moderate plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series, values start moderately high (around 0.2–0.35) and decrease toward a small positive value (but stay clearly above 0).
R2: Around the middle of the series, values rise steeply from this low level to a relatively high peak (about 0.6–0.75).
R3: After this main peak, values remain relatively high for a short interval, forming a broad high plateau or a second, slightly lower peak.
R4: Toward the end of the series, values decline from this high region but stay clearly above 0 (no long near‑zero “tail”).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first third of the series, values stay very close to 0 for several consecutive time points (a near‑flat, near‑zero baseline).
R2: From this near‑zero baseline, values then increase steeply to a relatively high peak (about 0.6–0.75) around the middle of the series.
R3: After the peak, values decrease more gradually, staying at mid‑range levels for a while rather than forming a second pronounced high peak.
R4: In the final part of the series, values fall back down close to 0 again, forming a noticeable near‑zero “tail” at the end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a medium value (about 0.2–0.35) and then gradually decreases.  
R2: It stays low (near 0–0.1) during the first third of the time (up to about index 7–8), forming a shallow “valley”.  
R3: After this valley it rises steeply to a single main peak around the middle (indices 11–14) with height about 0.6–0.75.  
R4: After the peak it decreases again toward the end, but the end level remains clearly above zero (around 0.15–0.45).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts very low, close to zero (about 0–0.05) and stays flat and near zero for the first quarter of the time (up to about index 5–6).  
R2: From this flat near-zero start it then increases smoothly, with no high early values before the rise.  
R3: It reaches a main peak slightly later than class 0 (around indices 13–16) with height about 0.6–0.75.  
R4: After the peak it falls back down and ends again close to zero (about 0–0.1).
